title: Implement PROP-068 — collapse workspace-sync's final steps into Step 4b bash block; inline cleanup; apply same pattern to sloppytoppy-score + sloppytoppy-rewrite (both currently DISABLED but pre-fixed for safe re-enable)
background:
PROP-068 (authored by tinker 2026-05-31T08:54Z in response to DIRECTIVE-005) verified all 6 operator findings from D31-005's amendment, completed the 13-agent audit, and proposed a 3-agent fix with 4 open questions for operator review. Operator has reviewed and provided answers below; this directive locks them in and commissions the implementation.

The primary insight, confirmed in the audit: workspace-sync leaks ~43% of its clones not because of disk failure / sandbox teardown / EXIT trap issues, but because the LLM skip-by-omission stops invoking bash blocks after Step 4b's run-report write (which is proven-invoked via report-existence across all 174 cycles in 9 days). The 250-line gap between Step 4b's last push and the trailing Cleanup block at line 1219 is the same skip-by-omission failure mode PROP-066 just closed for Step 4c. The fix is structural: collapse Steps 4a+4c+4b into one bash block, inline `rm -rf $CLONE` at the end of that block, and add a cleanup_ran field to the run-report so future audits can distinguish cleanup-ran from cleanup-skipped cycles.

The per-agent audit also identified two sibling agents at the same risk class (sloppytoppy-score, gap=71; sloppytoppy-rewrite, gap=40), both currently DISABLED since the 2026-05-21 workspace-sync mass-delete disaster. Operator approved simultaneous fix so they don't immediately leak when re-enabled. Three more agents (decider/tinker/structure-integrity) have cleanup-far-from-push but no in-prompt push to reorder against — they require a different fix shape and are NOT in PROP-068's scope. Operator additionally grep'd /sessions/*/dome-curmudgeon-*-clone and /sessions/*/curmudgeon-clone — zero orphans observed; curmudgeon family confirmed NOT leaking; excluded from PROP-068 fix.

Q_OP_1_files_committed_capture.question: After reorder (Step 4 → 4c → 4b), Step 4b's `files_committed` capture uses `git log -1 --name-only --pretty=format: HEAD | grep -c .` but HEAD will point to Step 4c's sentinel commit (~1 file) instead of Step 4's FUSE→git commit (0–500+ files). Options: (a) tmpfile capture before 4c, (b) grep-by-commit-message, (c) collapse 4a+4c+4b into one bash block so the variable stays local.
Q_OP_1_files_committed_capture.rationale: PROP-066 was specifically designed to escape cross-bash-session variable loss (option (a) reintroduces exactly that complexity via tmpfile). Grep-by-message (option (b)) is robust but couples the report-writer to the commit-message format which is fragile if anyone changes the commit prefix. Collapsing keeps the variable local, no cross-session juggling, no message-format coupling. The 32-line gap in workspace-sync is small enough to fit in one block without becoming unwieldy. Reuses the same architectural lesson PROP-066 already established (long trailing bash blocks → LLM skip-by-omission → unreliable execution). One block does everything ordering-sensitive: capture files_committed → run Step 4c helper → write run-report → push run-report → rm $CLONE.
Q_OP_1_files_committed_capture.implementation_note: The collapsed block ordering must be: (1) Capture files_committed counter from Step 4a's commit while it's still HEAD. (2) Invoke `node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js` (PROP-066 helper). (3) Write run-report JSON with files_committed=<captured>, cleanup_ran=true, and Step 4c's outcome propagated through. (4) git add + commit + push the run-report (this advances HEAD to a 2nd sentinel commit but that's fine — we already captured what we needed). (5) `rm -rf "$CLONE"` as the FINAL statement in the block. Optionally trap-protect the rm so a mid-block error still attempts cleanup (defense in depth — even though the bash block is small enough that trap-failure scenarios are unlikely).

Q_OP_2_cleanup_ran_field_honesty.question: `cleanup_ran=true` is a structural claim written before the actual rm runs. The interruption window between JSON write and rm is microseconds wide. Acceptable to keep `cleanup_ran=true`, or rename to `cleanup_about_to_run=true` for honesty, or verify-after-rm?
Q_OP_2_cleanup_ran_field_honesty.rationale: The rm failure mode is essentially impossible — $CLONE is owned by this UID, just created by this same cycle, no other process holds open file handles in scheduled-task land. The verified-after-rm pattern (rm, capture exit code, then update JSON) adds complexity for a theoretical concern. If we ever observe a leak DESPITE cleanup_ran=true (orphan exists with matching cycle ID), THAT's the bug to investigate; renaming the field preemptively just creates searchability noise (`cleanup_about_to_run` is harder to grep).

Q_OP_3_simultaneous_sloppytoppy_fix.question: Apply the same fix-shape simultaneously to sloppytoppy-score and sloppytoppy-rewrite (both DISABLED since 5/21 disaster)? Or wait until re-enabled?
Q_OP_3_simultaneous_sloppytoppy_fix.rationale: ~10 min per sibling agent is cheap. Both at the same risk class, both fixes are mechanical (one bash block reorder + one rm-inline + cleanup_ran reporting if a run-report mechanism exists). Doing it now eliminates a future forgetting risk — when sloppytoppy gets re-enabled (operator decision pending separately), it doesn't immediately start leaking. The marginal cost of fixing disabled prompts is just the prompt edit; runtime impact is zero until re-enable. Tradeoff favors apply-now.

Q_OP_4_curmudgeon_grep_first.question: For curmudgeon and curmudgeon-verify (INVESTIGATE-first per audit), check for actual orphans first or apply preventively?
Q_OP_4_curmudgeon_grep_first.answer: Grep-first complete: zero curmudgeon orphans observed. EXCLUDE curmudgeon family from PROP-068 fix.
Q_OP_4_curmudgeon_grep_first.rationale:
Operator ran `for d in /sessions/*/dome-curmudgeon-*-clone /sessions/*/curmudgeon-clone; do [ -d "$d" ] && ls -ld "$d"; done` and received no output. Bash glob no-match expands to the literal string and [ -d ] returns false → no output equals zero matching directories. The actual clone names from the curmudgeon prompts (`dome-curmudgeon-clone` per curmudgeon.md line 92, `dome-curmudgeon-verify-clone` per curmudgeon-verify.md line 71) DO match the glob, so this is a meaningful negative. Curmudgeon family confirmed NOT leaking. Excluded from PROP-068 fix.

DIAGNOSTIC IMPLICATION: The separate-trailing-cleanup-block pattern does NOT always leak. Curmudgeon has the same architectural pattern (per the audit) but doesn't leak. Possible reasons: (a) curmudgeon's prompt is shorter than workspace-sync's so LLM doesn't skip-by-omission, (b) curmudgeon's run cadence is lower so even occasional leaks haven't accumulated to operator-noticeable level, (c) curmudgeon's clone block is structured such that the LLM reliably reaches the Cleanup section. Tinker should NOT assume the workspace-sync leak pattern transfers automatically to all separate-trailing-cleanup-block agents — orphan observation is the load-bearing evidence.

explicitly_excluded_from_this_proposal: decider (different fix-shape per audit; no orphans observed-or-not)
poller (audit unclear; not in PROP-068 scope)
social (audit unclear; not in PROP-068 scope)
integrity (audit unclear; not in PROP-068 scope)
prune-integrity (audit unclear; not in PROP-068 scope)
scope_clarification.follow_up_scope_if_evidence_emerges: Operator will run a broader grep for dome-review-clean (shared decider/analyst clone), dome-tinker-clone, dome-integrity-clone, etc. If any orphans surface for those, file a separate directive (likely PROP-070 or amendment) — do NOT extend PROP-068 scope mid-implementation.

requested_work: Step 1 — Read PROP-068 + this directive's operator answers + verify the operator's diagnostic findings table from D31-005 (the 6 findings labeled finding_1 through finding_6 in the operator_followup_findings section) still match the actual workspace-sync.md state (line numbers may have shifted slightly since PROP-066 deployment refactored Step 4c; expected delta: Step 4c block shrank from ~140 lines to ~3 lines, so Cleanup block line number is now likely earlier than 1362).

Step 2 — Implement the PRIMARY FIX for workspace-sync.md (per Q-OP-1 answer (c) collapse): combine Step 4a (commit + push), Step 4c (PROP-066 helper invocation), Step 4b (run-report write + push), and Cleanup (rm) into one collapsed bash block at the end of the existing Step 4 section. Block ordering: capture files_committed → invoke sync-workspace-step4c.js → write run-report JSON (with files_committed=<captured>, cleanup_ran=true, and Step 4c outcome propagated) → git add + commit + push run-report → rm -rf $CLONE. Add a trap-EXIT inside the block (defense in depth) that attempts the rm if the block fails mid-flight.

Step 3 — Apply the same fix-shape to sloppytoppy-score.md and sloppytoppy-rewrite.md (per Q-OP-3 answer YES). Reorder + inline rm at end of the agents' final commit+push block. Add a safety guard (per PROP-068 audit recommendation): check `git status --porcelain` before rm and refuse to rm if uncommitted changes exist (analyst-baby has this pattern at line 225-229; mirror it). cleanup_ran reporting is conditional on a run-report mechanism existing (sloppytoppy agents may not have one — if not, document the gap and propose a minimal run-report for them in a separate follow-up).

Step 4 — Update the run-report JSON schema in monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-*.json to add `cleanup_ran: true` field. Update tinker's soft-complaints grep to flag any report with `cleanup_ran: false` or missing field. Existing reports (pre-deployment) will lack the field — that's expected; tinker should treat missing as 'pre-deployment legacy run, ignore' for runs older than the deployment timestamp.

Step 5 — Pre-deployment synthetic test (mirror PROP-066's 4-scenario test pattern): in a fresh sandbox, manually invoke the collapsed Step 4 bash block, verify (a) $CLONE is gone after the block completes, (b) run-report JSON has cleanup_ran=true, (c) files_committed has the correct count (not Step 4c's sentinel-only count). Capture results in `monitor/integrity/prop-068-synthetic-test-results-<ISO>.json`.

Step 6 — Deploy: commit + push all changes in one batch. Manually copy workspace-sync.md, sloppytoppy-score.md, sloppytoppy-rewrite.md to FUSE so the next scheduled cycle of each agent picks up the new prompts. (If PROP-066's Step 4c helper is working by now, this manual FUSE sync may be automatic via the next workspace-sync cycle — verify the PROP-066 canary first; if ≥1 sync-workspace-runs sentinel exists at top-of-hour, Step 4c is working and manual sync may be redundant.)

Step 7 — Post-deployment canary: 7-day observation window. Operator should observe ZERO new dome-sync-clone orphans in /sessions/. Tinker Mode 2 quick-check should grep workspace-sync reports for cleanup_ran=false count and flag if >0. If orphans persist past 7 days, file DIRECTIVE-008 for PROP-069-successor.

Step 8 — Lifecycle: mark this directive completed with prop_id_implemented='PROP-068' (or 'partially-implemented' if only workspace-sync ships and the sloppytoppy pair is deferred to a separate follow-up). Flip PROP-068.json status='implemented' with implementation_commit recorded. Update next_run_priorities to include the canary verification at 24h and 7d checkpoints.
non_goals: Do NOT extend PROP-068 scope mid-implementation to cover decider/tinker/structure-integrity/curmudgeon family — those are out of scope per operator's explicit scope_clarification above.
Do NOT touch DIRECTIVE-006 (PROP-069 narrative-cite recalibration) — that's a separate authoring directive still pending.
Do NOT pause scheduled tasks during deployment — same rationale as PROP-066's deploy (intended to be live, fallback is operator manual cleanup).

Do NOT add a run-report mechanism to sloppytoppy agents in THIS run — if they lack one, document the gap and propose in a separate follow-up directive. Don't expand scope to cover schema additions across multiple agents in one run.

Do NOT delete the existing Cleanup section header text from workspace-sync.md — just replace its body with a comment pointing to the collapsed Step 4 block. The Cleanup header is referenced from other docs (CLAUDE.md, possibly state-verification.md); leave a redirect stub for backward-compat.

context_pointers.prop_066_helper: monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js (deployed 2026-05-31T08:42Z via commit 29df258c; Step 4c is now a 3-line helper invocation in workspace-sync.md)
context_pointers.workspace_sync_md_post_prop_066: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md — Step 4c was at lines 1187-1344 (~157 lines) pre-PROP-066, now at lines 1191-1199 (~3 lines) post-PROP-066. The Cleanup block at line 1362-1370 may have shifted slightly. Re-verify line numbers in Step 1.
context_pointers.audit_methodology: PROP-068's per_agent_cleanup_audit section enumerated all 13 enabled scheduled agents and classified each by (a) does-it-clone, (b) clone-name, (c) gap-lines-between-push-and-cleanup, (d) operator-observed-orphans. Curmudgeon family grep'd by operator after PROP-068 authoring (this directive) and confirmed NOT leaking.
context_pointers.analyst_baby_safety_guard: monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md lines 225-229 implements the `git status --porcelain` safety guard before rm — mirror this pattern for sloppytoppy fixes.

expected_output: Commits on origin/main containing: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (collapsed Step 4 block + Cleanup stub redirect), monitor/prompts/sloppytoppy-score.md (reorder + inline rm + safety guard), monitor/prompts/sloppytoppy-rewrite.md (same), monitor/integrity/prop-068-synthetic-test-results-<ISO>.json (4-scenario synthetic test results), monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-068-*.json (status flipped to 'implemented' + implementation_commit recorded), monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-*.json (next cycle's report includes new cleanup_ran field). Plus this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-068'.
estimated_effort: ~45-60 min Opus. workspace-sync collapse is the bulk at ~25-30 min (careful prompt editing to preserve all semantic content while restructuring). Sloppytoppy fixes are ~10 min each (mechanical reorder + inline). Synthetic test ~10 min. Deploy + FUSE sync ~5 min.
priority_note: MEDIUM — same priority as PROP-068 authoring. The fix reduces operator's ~30 sec/week cleanup pattern to zero AND eliminates a class of skip-by-omission failures that may affect future agents. Combined with PROP-066's deployment, this morning's work closes two major architectural gaps in one day.
